config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse, censorship, or discrimination. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting basic rights first, then balancing them with the common good when there is a real and necessary reason. From my perspective, the state and the majority should not be trusted to decide everything for the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan. Hak individu perlu menjadi batas awal yang kuat, lalu baru dibatasi jika ada ancaman nyata dan proporsional, bukan hanya karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih nyaman. Kalau tidak, minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer akan paling cepat dirugikan. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan sambil tetap punya tanggung jawab bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “for the public good” can be used to pressure people into silence or conformity. At the same time, I also do not believe rights are absolute in every situation; during real emergencies, like public health crises or serious threats to safety, some limits can be justified if they are narrow and fair. The problem is that governments and majorities often expand those limits far beyond what is necessary, and that is where individual rights must remain the first line of defense. A society is stronger when it protects people from arbitrary power, not when it asks them to surrender their dignity for vague collective goals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena di Amerika, saya melihat “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan kebebasan individu dan terutama suara minoritas. Hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikalahkan hanya karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman atau pemerintah mengklaim sedang bertindak demi kebaikan bersama. Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan darurat yang sah, seperti krisis kesehatan atau ancaman keselamatan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu. Kalau tidak, masyarakat justru memberi terlalu banyak kuasa pada negara dan membuka pintu bagi penyalahgunaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia that idea is often used to justify pressure, conformity, and weak protections for dissent. At the same time, I do not support an absolute individualism either; in real emergencies like disease outbreaks or serious security threats, limited restrictions can be justified if they are transparent, proportional, and temporary. The key issue is that collective goals are too easily turned into a blank check for authorities and majorities, and once that happens, minorities and ordinary people pay the price. A good society is not one that demands sacrifice by default, but one that proves any sacrifice is truly necessary and tightly controlled.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena dari pengalaman saya di Amerika, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dijadikan alasan untuk menggerus kebebasan individu dan membungkam kelompok minoritas. Memang ada keadaan darurat yang sah, seperti krisis kesehatan atau ancaman keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus benar-benar sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih nyaman. Saya juga tidak percaya pada individualisme absolut; hak individu dan tanggung jawab sosial harus seimbang, tetapi hak harus menjadi batas awal yang kuat. Kalau tidak, negara dan mayoritas akan terlalu mudah memakai bahasa “demi kebaikan bersama” untuk melegitimasi penyalahgunaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
